e.v: Own a bank account with 10 million ingame money
Reach 10 hours of playing time in a single savegame
This animal type is not supported by the livestock trailer!

Chickens require a clean feeding area.
Animals always need water! They also require a clean feeding area, and some require straw.

A horse's value is determined by its fitness, health and cleanliness. They should be ridden every day to increase their fitness. Horses require straw for clean bedding and water and food to stay healthy. Periodically they need to be brushed and cleaned.
Feed them a mix of the following foods (for best results, provide all these types):

Make sure to buy a pallet of %s for this contract
Mow the grass in field %d. Then dry it with a tedder and bale it. Take the bales to %s.
Mow the grass in field %d. Then bale the grass and wrap it to make silage bales. Take the bales to %s.
Prepare field %d for the next crop by cultivating it.
Fertilize field %d with any type of fertilizer (liquid, solid, slurry or manure).

Plow field %d to loosen the soil and prepare it for the next crops.

You are planting the wrong crop for the contract
An additional tank to increase the capacity of your tools.
A place to keep your chickens.
A place to keep your cows.
A place to keep your horses.
A place to keep your pigs.
A place to keep your sheep.
Auger wagons (also known as chaser bins) are trailers that have their own screw conveyor. You can use them to store your crop and directly unload it into other trailers.
A bale collector eases the collection of bales.
A bale trailer is used to transport bales.
A bale wrapper packs grass bales in foil so they can ferment into silage.
A baler turns loose straw, grass or hay into convenient bales.
Produces honey which is sold automatically to earn you money.
Conveyor belts are an alternative way to load your trailers.
This conveyor belt transports material from one belt to the next one.
This belt will take material from the ground and load it onto the next conveyor belt.
Bigbags are used to refill your fertilizer spreaders.
Fresh oat to feed your horses with.
Bigbags are used to refill lime spreaders.
Perfectly balanced food pellets to feed your pigs.
Bigbags are used to refill your sowing machines.
Driving a car allows you to quickly travel from A to B.
Using a chainsaw you can cut down trees.
Buy coins to enrich your gameplay.
This machine can harvest the indicated fruit types.
Forage harvesters process the indicated fruit types.
For a harvester to work, it also needs a suitable header.
For a forage harvester to work, it also needs a suitable header.
A cultivator prepares fields for the next sowing.
Headers are attached to forage harvesters.
Header trailers can transport headers safely on the road.
Used to play with the dog.
A small hut often built in the shape of a house to shelter a dog.
A dolly serves as a link between a semitrailer and a tractor.
Silos are used to store crops
A forage mixer wagon blends silage, hay and straw into Total Mixed Ration (TMR) that can be fed to cows.
This machine takes straw bales and uses them to cover the ground of a cow stable with straw.
Fertilizer spreaders improve the harvest of your fields.
Liquid fertilizer can be poured into sprayers.
Seedling for poplars. Load this onto a tree planter.
With this pallet you can refill sowing machines for sugar cane.
A forage wagon collects grass, hay or straw. It can also be filled with chaff.
This machine can cut down trees.
This piece of machinery plants new trees into the ground. You can buy pallets of saplings at the shop to fill this planter.
This vehicle can transport tree logs.
Note: Very large trees cannot be processed
This machine breaks trunks into wood chips.
Front loaders are used to stack and load various goods.
Produces vegetables which are sold automatically to earn you money.
This harvester digs the potatoes out of the ground. Note that a haulm topper is needed prior to using this machine.
This harvester digs the potatoes out of the ground.
This self-propelled harvester collects potatoes in one go.
This self-propelled harvester collects sugar beets in one go.
This harvester digs the sugar beets out of the ground. Note that a haulm topper is needed prior to using this machine.
Before potatoes can be harvested, the foliage needs to be cut with a haulm topper like this one.
Before sugar beets can be harvested, the foliage needs to be cut with a haulm topper like this one.
Storage space for hay and straw.
Dropping woodchips at this selling station produces heat and earns you money.
Herbicides can be used in sprayers to remove weeds.
With this machine you can clean dirty vehicles and tools.
Levelers are used to move chaff and silage and level the heaps in the silos.
Liquid fertilizer tanks are used to refill your sprayers.
This trailer is used to transport animals.
This machine can collect sugar beets from the ground and tips them into a trailer.
This machine collects materials from the ground and tips them into trailers.
This trailer can transport other vehicles.
Manure spreaders improve the harvest of your fields.
Loading this machine with potatoes or sugar beets will create nicely packed pallets you can sell. Alternatively it can load conveyor belts with sugar beets or potatoes.
Any logs you put into this machine are converted into wood chips that are immediately sold to the sawmill.
With a plow you can prepare fields for the next sowing and create new fields.
Produces power which is sold automatically to earn you money.
This vehicle is equipped with reverse drive control.
With this machine you can restore the initial ground state (delete fields).
This pallet of saplings is used to fill the tree planter.
Semitrailers can be attached to a tractor by using a dolly.
Increases the storage capacity of your silos. Must be placed near your farm silos.
Skid Steer Loaders are used to stack and load various goods.
A field container is used as an interim storage for slurry. You can fill it with the other slurry tankers.
Slurry spreaders improve the harvest of your fields.
Liquid manure spreaders can be attached to suitable manure barrels.
With a seeder/planter you can sow fields.
Additionally, this machine offers the possibility to seed directly (no previous cultivating or plowing necessary).
Sprayers increase the harvest output of fields.
Additional tanks increase the capacity of sprayers.
Silos are used to store crops
A subsoiler prepares fields for the next sowing. It can be used instead of a plow.
Sugarcane trailers are able to tip their bins high in order to fill larger trailers directly.
A tedder turns grass around so it can dry faster.
Telehandlers are used to stack and load various goods.
Tippers/trailers are used to transport the harvest.
Tractors are required to pull trailers and tools.
This tractor can also attach a front loader.
Trailer with a 3-point hitch for tractors without one.
With a tree saw you can cut down trees.
The water station can supply your water tanks with fresh water.
Weeders pull out small grown weeds between crops, improving the field's state. They can only be used in the first growing stages of crops.
A weight helps keeping a tractor's wheels on the ground and improves its balance.
Wheel loaders are used to stack and load various goods.
A windrower arranges grass, hay or straw in rows (windrows), which can then be scooped up by forage wagons or balers.
This workshop allows you to create a spot anywhere on the map where you can customize your machinery without the need of a paid mechanic.
With these Bigbags you can refill your tools with seeds and fertilizer. Once they are empty you will have to buy new ones from the shop. You can find them in the objects category.
If a vehicle's gas tank is empty, you can refuel it at gas stations like this one. There are several of these found along the main roads.

These silos are for temporary storage of your commodities. Drive over the unloading spot with a full trailer and unload its contents. A symbol is displayed to the right as soon as you are in position to start unloading.
To retrieve a commodity from storage, position a trailer below the pipe on the other side and activate it. The trailer will be loaded until it is full or until the storage is empty.

As soon as you purchase some sheep, they'll start producing wool. Over time, a pallet with several sacks of wool will appear here. You can pick it up with a front loader and transport it to the spinnery where you can sell it.
If you want to sell wool to the spinnery, you can transport the pallets from the sheep pasture to this spot and place them inside this marker.
If you are carrying chicken eggs, you can sell them here by simply walking into the symbol.
You can sell wood logs here. Straight and long pieces without branches earn you more money than short and bent ones.
Here you may unload and sell your wood chips.

If you want to sell wood, you can simply drop it into the mill pond.
Straight and long pieces get you more money than short and bent ones.

To sow sugarcane you will need a billet planter like the Gessner Single Row Billet Planter. As with potatoes, the machines can be filled with sugarcane stalks of the last harvest. You can tip the stalks directly into the billet planter or make use of a telehandler or wheel loader with a shovel attached to fill the machine. Even without sugarcane stalks you can start to plant sugarcane. Simply purchase a pallet of sugarcane in the shop and fill the billet planter with it.
Sugarcane grows comparably slow, but it does not wither. Harvested sugarcane will grow back after a while and can be harvested again.
This is your one-stop destination for acquiring new machinery and for selling your old machines. Newly bought equipment always appears on the space next to this building.

To harvest sugarcane, you will need a sugarcane harvester. It removes the top leaves and cuts the thick sugarcane stalks into small, compact pieces. Just as forage harvesters, sugarcane harvesters do not have their own tank and it is required to accompany them with a tipper.

During harvest, specialized sugarcane tippers are commonly used as they have a higher tip height, allowing them to tip the sugarcane stalks directly into a truck to guarantee a quick transport. Sugarcane can't be stored in silos, however, it can be tipped anywhere on the ground and stored there. Different stations offer different prices for sugarcane, but the most profitable station would be the sugarcane mill.
Store your potatoes and sugar beets by unloading them here. Two conveyor belts are located behind the hall where you can fill your trailers.
If you have a surplus of straw or hay bales, you can place them in here and receive some money in return.

Drive to this tank to fill your sprayers or spreaders with fertilizer. During the filling process, money is deducted from your account.
You can find another tank at the garden center.
This facility provides heating to the nearby village. Once you start cutting trees and generate wood chips, you can sell them here.

Beside road vehicles you can also control trains.
At this place you can transfer goods from one train to the other by using the crane for logs or by loading/unloading crops from or to the storage building.
On the other side of the building is the loading station for trailers.
If you have the coin to spare, you can refill your sowing machines at these pallets. The same pallets can also be found at the garden center.
At fountains like these you can refill your water tanks.
Getting rid of Straw and Hay

In Farming Simulator 20, there are ten types of crop: wheat, barley, oat, cotton, canola, sunflowers, soybeans, corn, potatoes and sugar beet.
To process field crops, there are three mandatory steps: cultivating, sowing and harvesting.

Use a cultivator to aerate the ground before you start to sow your crop.
To cultivate, you must lower the cultivator into the ground.

With a seeder you can then plant your crop.
- Fill the seeder with seeds at your farm - Select the crop you want to sow - Activate the seeder

When the crop is ready to be harvested, use a combine harvester with a suitable header attached.
- Attach a header suited to the crop you will harvest - Turn on the harvester
Unload your harvest into a trailer. It can then be sold at a selling point or stored in your silo for later use.
Growing potatoes and sugar beet requires special sowing machines and harvesters.

To harvest cotton, you must use a self-propelled cotton harvester.
When the harvester tank is full, you must unload the large bale of cotton. You need a special trailer to transport it to a selling point.

To increase the yield of your field by 50%, you can fertilize it using either a:
- sprayer - fertilizer spreader - manure spreader - slurry tank
The sprayer and fertilizer spreader can be filled at your farm. The manure spreader and slurry tank can be filled at the cow pasture or pig enclosure.
Fodder is any product used specifically to take care of your animals. It includes: grass, hay and straw.

Grass is cut with a mower attached to a tractor.
Use a tedder to turn cut grass over so that it can dry properly and become hay. Hay looks slightly lighter and less dense than cut grass.
When you harvest wheat, barley or oat your harvester will drop straw.

To make grass and hay collecting easier, arrange it into rows using a windrower. Straw coming out of the harvester is already arranged in rows.
Then, it can be collected in a loading wagon or made into bales using a baler.

Grass can be fed to your cows and sheep. Hay can be fed to your cows, sheep and horses. Straw can be used as clean bedding for cows, pigs and horses.
Also, grass and hay can be made into chaff.

Chaff is made by chopping up a crop using a forage harvester. You can make chaff from corn, wheat, barley, oat, canola, soybeans, grass or hay.
But remember to attach a tipper because forage harvesters don't have internal tanks.
Chaff can be sold at the biogas station.

In Farming Simulator, you can also take care of animals: horses, cows, sheep and pigs.
You can purchase animals from your local animal dealer.

You must take the animals back to the farm in an animal trailer.
To do so, make sure that the trailer is parked close to the animal dealership.

You must take care of your animals: feed them, give them water and change their bedding using a straw blower.
When animals are happy, they reproduce and new animals are added to your livestock.

Food: grass, hay Product: milk, manure and slurry Reproduction speed: slow
To sell milk, first move a milk tank into the interactive zone to collect milk. Then go to a selling point that accepts milk and sell it.

Food: grass and hay Product: wool Reproduction speed: medium
In order to sell wool, move a trailer into the interactive zone to collect wool and transport it to the spinnery.

Food: corn, wheat, barley, soybeans, canola, sunflowers, potatoes and sugar beet. Product: manure and slurry Reproduction speed: fast
If you take care of your pigs, they will reproduce. Then they can be sold for a profit. Just use the animal trailer to take them to the animal dealer where you can sell them.

Food: hay and oat
Horses are trained and then sold for a profit. You can own up to eight horses, each with its own name and statistics. Fitness: the higher the fitness level, the higher the value. Raise their fitness level by riding them. Health: horses require straw for clean bedding and water and food to stay healthy.

As soon as cows and pigs are fed, they produce slurry, which is collected in a pit.
Put a slurry tank next to the pit to collect slurry and spread it over your fields to fertilize them.

Adding straw bedding to a cow or pig enclosure creates manure for the farm.
Put a manure spreader next to the manure heap to collect it. Spread it on your fields to fertilize them.

To sell your products, you need to load your products onto a trailer and drive to one of the selling points marked on the map. Open the prices window to see what each station accepts and at what price. Ideally, you want to sell your products at the highest paying station.

The prices of all sellable commodities change constantly in accordance with supply and demand. You can influence prices by deciding what you want to put up for sale and how much of it.
If, for example, you only sell wheat for several deliveries, demand will go down considerably and the price will fall accordingly. This also means that prices for commodities you hardly ever sell will go up much faster.

Since the unloading stations are competing with each other, a station may detect a sudden need for a certain commodity. In that case, a temporary demand peak starts.
During demand peaks, the unloading station in question offers a higher price than usual. You will receive a message as soon as peak demand starts and you can look up current demand in the Prices menu.
You can purchase new vehicles and tools from the shop. Equipment is divided into categories or brands.
In the Vehicle Overview menu, you can see all the equipment you currently own. You can also sell equipment from here. The current value depends on age and usage.

Here are the explanations for each icon related to vehicles:
(1) Engine power (2) Required power (3) Fuel tank (4) Capacity (5) Working width (6) Maximum speed (7) Maximum working speed (8) Crop types (9) Seed types (10) Compatibility (11) Age (12) Operating hours (13) Owned Items

You can hire a computer-controlled AI helper for the following tasks: cultivating, sowing, harvesting, fertilizing, mowing, tedding and windrowing.
A hired worker is displayed with a small hexagon and a letter on the mini map. Once your helper finishes processing the field, they will stop automatically. Hiring a helper will cost you money as long as the helper is active.
To get the best out of your workers: - start in the corner of a field - make sure there won't be anything in their way - don't put too many workers in the same field

In the map overview, you can check the state of your fields, crop types, growth status and soil composition.
You can also buy new fields, reset your tools and vehicles if they get stuck or place a beacon at a location, depending on which icon you have selected.
In Farming Simulator 19 there are 13 types of crops: wheat, barley, canola, sunflowers, soybeans, corn, oat, oilseed radish, potatoes, sugar beet, poplars, sugarcane and cotton.
To process field crops, there are 3 mandatory steps: cultivating, sowing and harvesting.
Use a plow or cultivator to aerate the ground before you start to sow your crops.
To cultivate, you must lower the cultivator into the ground.

You can sow using a seeder or a planter.
To sow, you must: - Fill the machine with seeds from a seed pallet - Select the crop you want to sow - Turn on the machine - Lower it into the ground
Seed pallets can be purchased at the shop. Some seeders can cultivate and sow in one go. Check the shop description to find a machine that can do this.

When the crop is ready to be harvested, use a combine harvester with a suitable header attached.
To harvest, you must: - Unfold the harvester (and the header if required) - Turn it on
Unload your harvest into a trailer. It can then be sold at the associated place or stocked for a later use.
The cultivation of potatoes and sugar beets requires specialized sowing machines and harvesters.
The easiest and fastest way of harvesting potatoes or sugar beets is to use a self-propelled harvester. However, a machine like this is quite costly and likely to exceed your budget early on.

A cheaper alternative is to use a tractor with several specialized tools. This basically works in two steps: once the plants are fully grown, you have to cut the foliage with a haulm topper, like the Grimme KS 75-4.
After that, you can get the crop out of the ground by using a harvester, like the Grimme SE 260.
For sowing cotton, you need a planter. To fill it, use a seed pallet. To harvest cotton, you must use a self-propelled cotton harvester.
When the harvester tank is full, you must unload the large bale of cotton. You need a special trailer to move it to the selling point.

Oilseed radish is a special crop, as you don't harvest it at the end, but dig it in. Once the plants are visible, you can use a cultivator on your field. It's a so-called green manure and works as a fertilizer, increasing your next yield by 30%.

There are many ways you can take care of your field to increase its yield.
To obtain a 65% yield bonus you must: - Spread fertilizer twice (+ 25% each time) - Spread lime every 3 harvests (+15%)
And to avoid a 35% yield malus, you must: - Remove bad weeds (otherwise -20%) - Plow after corn, potatoes, sugar beets and sugarcane (otherwise -15%)
Grass is cut with a mower, which is usually attached to a tractor.
Use a tedder to turn the cut grass around so it can dry properly into hay. Hay looks slightly lighter and less saturated than cut grass.
To make grass, hay or straw collecting easier, it is recommended to arrange it into rows by using a windrower.
Grass can be collected with a loading wagon or made into grass bales using a baler.

Grass can be: - Fed to your animals - Sold at a selling point - Made into silage (by putting cut grass in a clamp silo or by wrapping a bale)
Hay can be: - Fed to your animals - Sold at a selling point - Made into Total Mixed Ration
When you harvest wheat, barley or oat you can set your harvester to create straw swaths.

Straw swaths can be packed into bales by using a baler. Alternatively, straw can be picked up by loading wagons. You can sell it directly, use it as bedding for cows or use it as a component for Total Mixed Ration.

Chaff is made by chopping up a crop using a forage harvester. You can make chaff from corn, wheat, barley or canola. Make sure to: - Use a header that's suitable for the crop you're chopping - Attach a tipper because forage harvesters don't have internal tanks
Chaff is used to make silage.

To turn chaff or grass into silage, you need to: 1) Dump it into a bunker silo (at the biogas plant or in one of your own silos). 2) Compact the heap by driving over it. The silo must be at least 10% full. 3) Cover the silo and start the fermenting process. 4) When fermentation has finished, uncover the silo.

Afterwards, you can cover the silo and start the fermenting process. The resulting silage can be picked up with a front loader equipped with a silage fork and dumped into a mixer-wagon or directly into a feeding trough.
You can also directly make silage from a grass bale by using a bale wrapper.

You can optimize the milk production of your cows by feeding them Total Mixed Ration (TMR). TMR is produced by mixing hay, straw and silage together in a mixer wagon.
It's simple, put into the mixer wagon: - 2 hay bales. - 1 straw bale. - Fill the rest with silage.

If you want to take a break from harvesting, or if that isn't your main activity, you can also take care of animals: horses, cows, sheep, pigs and chickens.
First you need to buy an animal pen in the shop. Then you can purchase animals from your local animal dealer.

You can bring the animals back to the farm yourself in an animal trailer. To do so, make sure that the trailer is parked close to the animal dealer.
For a fee, the animal dealer can deliver the animals directly to your farm.

Food: hay and oat
Horses are trained and then sold for a profit. You can own up to 16 horses, each with its own name and fitness level. The higher the fitness level, the higher the value. Raise their fitness level by riding them.
When you buy a horse it is given a name automatically. You can change its name in the animal menu.

You must take care of your animals: feed them, give them water and change their bedding by using a straw blower.
When animals are happy enough, they reproduce and new animals are added to your animal population.

Food: corn, wheat, barley, canola, sunflowers, soybeans, potatoes and sugar beets. Product: manure and slurry Reproduction speed: fast
If you take care of your pigs, they will reproduce. Then they can be sold for a profit.

Food: grass, hay, silage and TMR Product: milk, manure and slurry Reproduction speed: slow
Total Mixed Ration (TMR) is the best food for cows and will maximize their milk production.
As soon as cows are fed, they not only produce milk but slurry as well, which is collected either in a slurry tank or a pit. Note that pigs also produce slurry when they're fed.
Adding straw bedding to a cow or pig enclosure creates manure for the farm.
Food: grass and hay Product: wool Reproduction speed: fast
In order to sell the wool, you have to grab the pallet at the sheep pasture with a pallet fork and transport it to the spinnery.

Food: wheat Product: eggs
Pallets of eggs appear next to the chicken pen. You can move these pallets by hand. Put them in your pick-up and sell them at a selling point.
New trees do not just pop up by themselves, they need to be planted with a specialized planting machine.

Purchase a pallet of either pine or poplar saplings from the shop and load it onto the Damcon PL 75 tree planter. Pine trees can be planted almost anywhere and will take a while to grow. Plant the poplars on a field and harvest them with a forage harvester and a specialized header once they are fully grown.

Fully grown trees can be logged and cut into pieces either by using a chainsaw or a tree harvester. If you cut the tree with a chainsaw, you will need to remove small branches manually. The tree harvester can do it in one go. The resulting logs can be delivered and sold to the sawmill.
The price of wood not only depends on the length but also on the straightness of the delivered pieces. Small, bent pieces will generally sell for less than long straight ones.
You can also chop up logs with a wood chipper to make wood chips and deliver them to the sawmill.
You can harvest poplars with the help of a forage harvester and a special header in conjunction with a trailer to obtain a plethora of woodchips.
The remaining stumps can be removed with a stump grinder.
Small objects, such as logs, can be picked up by hand and either dropped or thrown. A grabbing hand icon will appear in the middle of your screen if you focus on an object that can be lifted.
In Farming Simulator you can produce a lot of things. To help you keep track of everything here, you will find a quick recap of all the possible productions in the game.
Wheat, barley, canola, corn, sunflower, soybean and oat: these grains can be harvested when a field is ready.
You can harvest cotton and sugarcane with a special harvester.
Wheat, barley, canola, corn, sunflowers, soybeans and oat: when a field is ready you can harvest these grains.
Grass can be cut with a mower. It can be gathered with a loading wagon.
Hay is grass dried with a tedder, it can then be made into bales.
Straw can be created while harvesting wheat or barley with a dedicated harvester.
Chaff can be produced from wheat, barley, canola or corn by using a chopper.
Silage can be created by fermenting chaff in a clamp silo. It can also be produced by wrapping round grass bales with a bale wrapper.
Total Mixed Ration is produced by mixing together hay, straw and silage, with at least 25% of each and no more than 75% of each.
Liquid Fertilizer and Fertilizer can be bought in a large tank or bag from the shop.
Digestate can be produced by delivering silage to the biogas plant.
Lime can be bought in pallets at the shop.
Herbicide can be bought in pallets at the shop.
Manure is produced by cows and pigs as long as the cows are provided with straw bedding.
Slurry is produced by cows and pigs as long as the cows are fed.
Logs are produced by cutting down trees and by cutting the branches with a chainsaw or a dedicated tool.
Wood chips are produced by processing cut logs in a wood chipper. You can also chop poplars with a forage harvester.
Milk is produced by your cows. Their productivity is linked to how well you take care of them.
Sheep produce wool that is accumulated into sacks, which can be sold directly for money if they are fed on grass.
Chickens produce eggs when they're fed. Pallets of eggs appear next to the chicken pen.
If your pigs are happy, they'll reproduce. You can sell your pigs to the livestock dealer to earn money.

To sell your products, you need to load your products - normally onto a tipper or trailer - and drive to one of the selling points marked on the map. Open the prices window to identify which station accepts what products and at what price. Ideally, you would want to sell your products at the highest paying station.

The prices of all sellable commodities change constantly and act in accordance with supply and demand. You can influence the prices by deciding what you want to deliver and how much of it.
If, for example, you sell only wheat in several deliveries, its demand will go down considerably and its price will fall accordingly. This also means that prices for commodities you hardly ever sell will go up much faster.

Since the unloading stations are competing with each other, a station may detect a sudden need for a certain commodity. In that case, a temporary demand peak starts.
During demand peaks, the unloading station in question offers a higher price than usual. You will receive a message as soon as peak demand starts and you can look up all current demands in the Prices menu.

The fields on the map are owned by other farmers controlled by the computer.
They take care of their fields but are always in need of assistance. To start a job for them, go to the Contracts menu. They can lend you any tools you need, but you can also use your own. You can cooperate with other players in multiplayer mode to complete these jobs.

You can only work on land you own: you can't cut down trees or create new fields on other people's land.
To buy a new parcel, go to the map overview menu and switch to the land screen.

You can purchase new vehicles and tools from the shop. Visit the shop directly via the map or press the designated key (standard P) to open the shop window at any time. Equipment is divided into categories or brands. Change the view using the top arrow buttons.
You can purchase new vehicles and tools from the shop.
You can purchase new vehicles and tools from the shop.
You can purchase new vehicles and tools from the shop.

Certain vehicles can be customized when bought. Each of them has different customization possibilities.
To customize them later, bring the vehicle to the marked area in front of the shop, then interact with the shop symbol.

The following items can be customized: vehicle color and design, engine and wheel setup and the option to have a front loader attacher installed or not. Note that not every vehicle or tool allows for every customization.

From the shop window, you can also enter your garage. You will see all the vehicles you currently own there and you can sell them from there. Their current value depends on their age and usage.
In front of the shop, there's a marked area where you can park vehicles and tools in order to sell them. Anything you sell this way nets you 20% more money of the selling price since you went to the trouble of driving the vehicle or tool all the way to the shop.

Over time, your vehicle will deteriorate. Vehicles in need of repair lose speed or engine power.
Take the vehicle to the shop or to your vehicle workshop to pay for repairs.

If you want to try new vehicles/tools, you have the possibility to lease them instead of buying them.
In order to do so, you can go to the shop and press the lease button instead of the buy button. You will be asked to pay a starting fee, then you will be charged by the hour. The price you pay is linked to the customizations you select.
Note that you can't customize a vehicle after leasing it.
The icons in the shop and garage have different meanings. If you find yourself lost, you can find an explanation below:
1) Income per hour: Shows the amount of money you gain per hour.
2) Engine power: Shows the power of the engine.
3) Required power: Shows the required engine power to operate the tool properly.
4) Fuel tank: Shows the maximum capacity of the fuel tank.
5) Capacity: Shows how much the vehicle tank can contain.
6) Owned: Shows how many vehicles/tools of this type you currently own.
7) Leased: Shows how many vehicles/tools of this type you are currently leasing.
1) Working width: Shows the working width of the vehicle/tool.
2) Maximum speed: Shows the maximum speed of the vehicle or in case of tools, the maximum speed it can be operated with.
3) Fruit types: Shows with which crops it can be filled or which crops it can process.
4) Seed types: Shows which seeds it can sow.
6) Information: Gives you a general explanation of the vehicle/tool and important information.
There are only 2 icons in the garage that don't appear in the store:
7) Age: Shows how many days you've owned the vehicle/tool.
8) Operating hours: Shows the operating hours of the vehicle/tool (based on real time).
9) Maximum working speed: Shows the maximum speed the vehicle can work at on even ground.
You can hire a computer-controlled AI helper for the following tasks: - Cultivating - Sowing - Harvesting - Fertilizing - Mowing - Tedding - Windrowing

A hired worker is displayed with a small hexagon and a letter on the mini map. Once your helper finishes processing the field, they will stop automatically. Hiring a helper will cost you money as long as the helper is active.
You can hire multiple helpers at the same time, but make sure to keep an eye on their costs!

To get the best out of your workers: - Make them start in the corner of a field. - Make sure there won't be anything in their way. - Don't put too many workers in the same field. - For work related to grass, make sure it takes place in the fields.

On the finance screen, you can see all income and expenditures for the current day and the previous 4 days. The loan you have taken from the bank is also displayed.
If you need more money urgently, you can borrow some from the bank in loans of $CURRENCY_SYMBOL5000. However, at the end of each day, you will have to pay excessive interest on the loans you have taken out.

The weather affects what you can do in the game. While there is hail or rain, you can't harvest your crops and will have to wait until it stops. Keep an eye on the weather forecast icon in the top right corner. When 2 weather icons are displayed, the right one will show you what the weather will be in 6 hours.
The train is available as any other vehicle but won't cost you a maintenance fee. You can use it to load a lot more of your produce and move it across the map.
Cranes will help you load and unload goods on your train. Note that you can't move animals by train; use the animal trailer in order to do so.

To check the state of your fields, use the map overview. Here you can see: - Crop types in each field - Growth status - Soil composition
You can change the view mode for each field too.

There is also an overview showing the selling points and your machinery on this page. Select an icon to: - Reset your tools and vehicles if they get stuck - Place your vehicles or visit a location - Place a beacon at a location
1) Vehicles such as tractors, trucks or cars
4) Tools such as cultivators, plows or tedders.
You will find a general overview of the map symbols below:
1) You can sell your crops here. Check the price menu to see what crops each place wants.
2) You can sell your grass here.
3) This place has a great demand: they are paying well for a type of crop.
5) Buy or sell your animals here.
10) You can purchase various goods here.

Farming Simulator can be changed to your likings with community-created modifications (mods). They can alter your way of playing greatly, make things easier or even more complex and realistic. They can add new machines and tools, placeable objects or even completely new maps to play on. To browse through the mods, visit the mod section in the game's main menu.
When transporting large objects like tree trunks or bales it is important to secure your cargo. Some trailers allow you to fasten tension belts in order to transport the objects safely.

Once you have loaded your cargo you can fasten all tensions belts directly from your vehicles controls. Alternatively you can fasten each belt manually by standing to the side of the trailer next to your cargo. A light green outline will show you where the tension belt can be fastened.
Sugarcane differs from typical field and root crops. It needs more time to mature but it also cannot wither. You need special sowing and harvesting equipment for sugarcane.

To sow sugarcane you will need a billet planter like the Gessner Single Row Billet Planter. As with potatoes, the machines can be filled with sugarcane stalks of the last harvest. You can tip the stalks directly into the billet planter or make use of a telehandler or wheel loader with a shovel attached to fill the machine. Even without sugarcane stalks you can start to plant sugarcane. Simply purchase a pallet of sugarcane in the shop and fill the billet planter with it.
Sugarcane grows comparably slow, but it does not wither. Harvested sugarcane will grow back after a while and can be harvested again.

To harvest sugarcane, you will need a sugarcane harvester. It removes the top leaves and cuts the thick sugarcane stalks into small, compact pieces. Just as forage harvesters, sugarcane harvesters do not have their own tank and it is required to accompany them with a tipper.

During harvest, specialized sugarcane tippers are commonly used as they have a higher tip height, allowing them to tip the sugarcane stalks directly into a truck to guarantee a quick transport. Sugarcane can't be stored in silos, however, it can be tipped anywhere on the ground and stored there. Different stations offer different prices for sugarcane, but the most profitable station would be the sugarcane mill.

As sugarcane regrows, tillage is nearly unnecessary. If you play with 'periodic plowing required', the ground must be aerated after 3 harvests to secure a full yield. The best results are achieved with a subsoiler like the KUHN DC 401, but a plow will be sufficient as well. After loosening the ground, you can plant new stalks of sugarcane.
To earn money, you need to sell what you produce (crops, animals and their products).
When it rains or hails you can't harvest crops, you must wait until the weather improves.
Don't know how to do something? Check the Help section in the Pause menu.
You can increase the yield of your harvest by fertilizing and spraying your fields after sowing.
If your animals have water, food and a clean stable, you can soon expect offspring.
Pigs can eat nearly everything - corn, wheat, barley, canola, sunflowers, soybeans, potatoes and sugar beet.
Grass is cut with a mower, which is usually attached to a tractor. Afterwards, you can use a tedder to turn the cut grass over and dry it to make hay.
Horses need hay, water and oat to be happy.
Ride your horses to train them. By training them, their value increases.
Using a bale wrapper, you can turn grass bales into silage bales.
To earn money, you need to sell what you produce (crops, wood, animals and their products).
When there's rain or hail you can't harvest your crops, you must wait until the weather improves.
If a vehicle or tool gets stuck, you can reset it in the shop parking lot on the map overview screen.
Don't know how to do something? Check the Help section in the pause menu.
Change your environment! You can transform a forest into fields if you want to: cut down the trees, remove the stumps and use a plow to create a field.
Note that you can carry around small pieces of wood by hand if they're not too heavy.
If your front loader is carrying something heavy, you can attach a weight to the back of the tractor to balance it out.
You can increase the yield of your harvest by fertilizing and spraying your fields after seeding.
Pigs can eat nearly everything - corn, wheat, barley, canola, sunflowers, soybeans, potatoes and sugarbeet.
If your animals have water, food and a clean stable, you can soon expect offspring.
Some vehicles allow for additional customization, such as different wheels, a stronger engine, a different color or a front loader attachment.
Before you can harvest potatoes or sugarbeet, you must remove the foliage on top.
You can fertilize a field with manure, slurry or manufactured fertilizer (liquid or solid).
You can buy new land for growing crops in the map menu. You can also sell land you own if necessary.
You cannot work, mow grass or cut trees on someone else's land. You need to buy the land.

On the finance screen, you can see all the income and expenditure for the current day and previous four days. If you need more money urgently, you can borrow some from the bank in increments of $CURRENCY_SYMBOL5000.

Selling wood is a profitable business and can be a welcome change from regular field work. You can cut down trees with a chainsaw or a tree harvester. They should be debranched and cut into logs before they're sold.
When selling logs, the length of a piece and its suitability for processing (the less curvature the better) determines its value.
Grass is cut with a mower, which is usually attached to a tractor. Afterwards, you can use a tedder to turn the cut grass over and dry it to make hay.
To make it easier to collect grass, it's best to arrange it into rows using a windrower. Grass can be collected with a loading wagon or turned into bales with a baler.
Horses need hay, water and oat to be happy.
Ride your horses to train them. By training them, their value increases.

Most of the fields on the map are owned by other farmers controlled by the computer. They take care of their fields but are always in need of assistance and can hire you as a contractor. Check the job board in the Contracts menu.
You can use a plow to create new fields on land you own.
A seeders and planters needs seeds. You can buy seed bags from the shop.
When you harvest wheat, barley or oat, you can create straw swaths with the harvester.
Straw swaths can be packed into bales using a baler. Alternatively, straw can be collected in loading wagons. It can be used as bedding for animals or to make Total Mixed Ration (TMR).

Your cows always produce milk, but you can improve their productivity by feeding them well. The best food you can give your cow is Total Mixed Ration (TMR), which is produced by mixing hay, straw and silage in a mixer wagon.
New trees don't just pop up by themselves. They need to be planted with a special planting machine. The shop can provide you with seedling pallets.
You can also crush logs using a wood chipper to sell them as wood chips.
Bring your vehicle to the shop or to your vehicle workshop for repairs.
If your vehicle is dirty, use a pressure washer to clean it.

To harvest cotton, you must use a self-propelled cotton harvester. When the harvester tank is full, you must unload the large bale of cotton. You need a special trailer to transport it to the selling point.

A transport fee is applied if you do not transport the animals with your own animal trailer!

Horses require straw for a clean bedding, water and food (consisting of 50% hay and 50% oat) to stay healthy. They should ride out every day to increase their fitness. Periodically they need to be brushed down and cleaned. A horse's value is determined by its fitness, health and cleanliness.
Animals always need water! They also require a clean feeding area and some require straw. The food mixture consists of:
Show the controls information box in the top left section of the screen.

If you'd like to start the guided tour later, start a new career and you will be asked again.
Well done! This is the end of the tour. You know the basics of farming now. Grow your crops, sell them, buy better tools… Good luck and have fun in the world of farming!

Well done! This is the end of the tour. You know the basics of farming now. Grow your crops, sell them, buy better tools… Good luck and have fun in the world of farming!
To learn about other activities, press the + Button and go to the Help section.

This is one of the fields you own at the start of the game. The wheat has conveniently grown to a stage where it can be harvested. Use your combine harvester to harvest the field.
Activate it so the header starts rotating.

This is one of the fields you own at the start of the game. The wheat has conveniently grown to a stage where it can be harvested. Use your combine harvester to harvest the field.
Press the A Button to activate the harvester.

Very good! Drive towards the field to start harvesting the wheat.
Use the accelerator on the bottom right and the steering on the left, or tilt your device if gyroscope steering is active.

Very good! Drive towards the field to start harvesting the wheat.
Use the Left Stick to steer and the ZR Button to accelerate.

The helper will now work on this task until they are done or the harvester's grain tank is full. You can now take care of other business.

The helper will now work on this task until they are done or the harvester's grain tank is full. You can now take care of other business.
Switch to the next tractor by pressing the R Button.

So far so good! Usually you'll have a lot to do, so it makes sense to hire helpers to work on your farm.
Whenever a job can be performed by a helper, it will be indicated in the bottom panel.
Hire a helper to take care of the rest of the field.

So far so good! Usually you'll have a lot to do, so it makes sense to hire helpers to work on your farm.
Whenever a job can be performed by a helper, it will be indicated in the bottom panel.
Press the Y Button to hire a helper. They will take care of the rest of the field.

Fields that have been harvested need to be cultivated before they can be sown again. This field has been harvested recently and now needs somebody to cultivate it.
Lower the cultivator by using the activation button.

Lower the cultivator by pressing the A Button.

Nice! Now activate the vehicle to turn on the seeder and start planting seeds in the soil.
You can select the type of crop you want to plant by tapping the seed icon.

Fields that have been cultivated are ready to be sown. For this you need to use a seeder.
Drive backwards towards the seeder. It will attach to the back of your vehicle automatically.

Welcome to Farming Simulator!
Would you like to start a short guided tour?
This will show you the basics of the game.
If you'd like to start the guided tour later, start a new career in Ravenport and you’ll be asked again.

And that's the end of this tour! Look out for more question marks that provide information about specific game elements or visit the tutorials found in the main menu for more in-depth information. Good luck and have fun in the world of farming!
If you’re not familiar with the basics of the game yet, we recommend going through the first two tutorials or taking the guided tour of Ravenport.

You have enough money to start the farming business of your dreams. What will your specialty be? Arable farming, breeding livestock, forestry or a bit of everything? The choice is yours!
First, buy some land, buildings and the necessary tools.
Then sell your products and expand your farm!

You only have enough money to start a small farm.
First, buy some land, buildings and the necessary tools.
Then sell your products and expand your farm!

OK! Attach the harvester's header that's sitting right in front of it.
The help panel in the top left corner always shows you the functions that are currently available.
This is one of the fields you own from the very start. Its wheat has conveniently grown to a stage where it can be harvested. Time for you to enter this combine harvester and get started!

The helper will now take care of this job until they are done or the grain tank of the harvester is full. You can now take care of other business. Exit the harvester and walk to the next question mark symbol indicated on the map.

Follow the path to the big yellow harvester at the edge of your field and touch the question mark icon next to it.
In the lower left is your map. The flashing green ring indicates where the question mark is located.

Now you're all set to harvest! Drive forward into the wheat. You can either adjust your vehicle's speed manually or use the cruise control to maintain a constant speed.
The maximum speed you can drive is automatically determined by the vehicle and attached tool you are using.

So far so good! Usually, however, you'll have a lot to do, so it makes sense to hire helpers on your farm.
Whenever a job can be executed by a helper, it will be indicated in the top left panel. Hire a helper to take care of the rest of the field.
Good! Now unfold the harvester and turn it on so the header starts rotating.
Well done! That's enough for now. You can finish the field later if you want. Or hire another helper to finish this job.

Fields that have been harvested need to be cultivated before they can be sown anew. This field has been harvested recently and now needs somebody to cultivate it. Enter the tractor and attach the cultivator that's placed behind it.

Let's switch to one of the other tractors. Using the switching feature you can quickly cycle through all of your vehicles.
Do this until you reach the tractor with the next question mark right beside it. Then get out and touch it.
Now lower the cultivator and drive over the field to cultivate its soil.
Fields that have been cultivated are ready to be sown. For this you need to use a sowing machine. Get back in the tractor and attach the sowing machine that's placed behind it.
Nice! You've got the hang of that. You can continue later if you want, or hire another helper for this job. Switch to the next tractor with a question mark icon next to it.

The sowing machine you just attached is already filled with seeds. Once you run out, you will have to refill it with a seed bag. You start with 2 bags at your farm. Once they've been used up, you'll need to buy more from the shop.
Time to sow! Select the seed you want to use, turn on and lower the sowing machine, then drive over the field.

Drive next to the harvester on its left-hand side until your trailer is alongside it. The pipe will start extending and if you are in the correct position, the wheat will start pouring out automatically.

Nicely done! Selling your crops is the main way to earn money as a farmer.
Let's drive over to the shop, where the final question mark icon is located. The flashing green ring indicates it on your map once again.
As soon as the harvester's tank is full (or even before that), you can unload its contents into a trailer. Hop on the tractor and attach the trailer to it.
This unloading station is one of the places on the map where you can sell your harvest. You can check the current prices of all products by opening the menu and going to the prices screen.
Move the tractor so the trailer is located right over the pit. As soon as the unload symbol appears at the bottom of the screen, you can unload the wheat and sell it.
Great! Wheat is being dumped into your trailer. Wait until it is done and then drive your tractor with the trailer to the spot indicated on the map and touch the question mark there.

This is where you can sell your vehicles and tools or buy new equipment. You can either visit the shop in person by activating the shopping symbol in front of it or trigger it anywhere by pressing the button shown below.

Welcome to Farming Simulator!
Would you like to start a short guided tour?
This will show you the basics of the game.

The next step is to keep them happy.
Let's feed them.

Good! They’ve got a nice clean feeding area, straw to lie down on and enough water and food to keep them happy and productive!
They reproduce too: From time to time, a new animal will appear.
In the next lesson, you’ll learn how to make up a Total Mixed Ration, the best thing to feed your cows.

Cleanliness around the trough is always recommended.
As your animals eat, they spill their food. It has to be cleaned from time to time or it will impact the animals' productivity.
Learn how to purchase pigs and feed them properly.

Now that the pigs are in your trailer, drive them to their pen.
The location is marked with a flashing icon on the map. Yellow and black stripes mark the area where you should park your trailer.

Now, get into the telehandler and attach the bale fork to the front.
Pierce the straw bale in front of you, lift it up and drop it into the straw blower.

Animals are bought from the animal dealer. There’s one on each map.
First, we’re going to buy pigs and transport them to their enclosure.
Enter the tractor, attach the weight and the animal trailer to it.

Enter in the telehandler and attach the shovel.
Feed the pigs with the four piles of feed in front of the telehandler. Pick them up one after another and drop them into the trough.

Now get in the tractor and attach the weight and the straw blower.
Then drive to the bedding area marked on your map and distribute the straw.
Be careful with the orientation of the straw blower.
Enter the telehandler and attach the shovel. Then clean up any spilled food and put it back in the trough.

Good work! Feeding animals keeps their productivity up.
The higher their productivity, the more you animal products you’ll obtain. Such as wool from sheep or milk from cows.
Now, let's give them some water.

Each animal eats differently.
Pigs eat almost anything, but they still need to have a balanced diet.
They need: 1) Maize, as their basic food 2) Grain like wheat or barley 3) Protein from canola or soybeans 4) Root crops like potatoes or sugar beet

To fill the water trailer, you have to drive to a water tower. There is one at the location marked on your map.
The water is free so don't hesitate to fill your water tank right up.
Now that the tank is full, return to the pigpen and fill the water trough.

To interact with the animal dealer, park the trailer in the area marked on the ground.
Then open the animal dealer menu. This is where you can buy or sell your animals.
Buy as many pigs as you can fit in your trailer.

Great!
Finally, let's clean around the animal trough.

Straw bedding is needed for pigs, cows and horses.
It improves your animals' productivity. Also, the straw will become manure over time.
Open the pigpen menu and unload the pigs from your trailer into their new home.

Nice job! Food and water are essential for animal productivity. Check that they have enough of both regularly.
Some animals also need straw for bedding.

Water is vital for animals. Without water, your animals will stop being productive.
Let's get in the tractor and attach the weight and the water tank.

Today, you’ll learn how to take care of your animals.
Animals need: 1) Food 2) Water 3) Straw bedding (for pigs, cows and horses) 4) Clean troughs
In this lesson, you'll learn to take care of pigs.
Good work! And with this you are now prepared to turn your land into a thriving farm!
Learn the basics of the game and how to effectively work your land.

You need to attach the weight and the cultivator.
First, slowly drive to the small black weight in front of you and attach it to your tractor.
Then, move backwards toward the cultivator and attach it to the back of your tractor.
The extra weight will counter balance the heavy weight at the back.

Cultivating aerates the ground before you start sowing your crops.
You’ll need a tractor and cultivator for this task.
The flashing icon on your map shows you where to unload the tipper. Drive over there and empty the grain into your silos.
Enter the combine-harvester and attach the header at the front.
Here's your tractor! Tractors are required to pull tippers and tools. Hop in!
Get in the tractor and attach the tools as you did before.
Great! Get out of the combine and get into the tractor in front of the tipper.
Right then! Tractor. Plow. Weight. Let's go!
Nicely done, you've got the hang of cultivation! Ready to plant some seeds?

Excellent! The grain will keep well in your silos. There is a limited capacity you can store in them but you will be able to expand them later in the game.
Once you are ready to sell your harvest, you can take the grain out of your silos and deliver them to one of the various selling stations in the world to make some money!
Good job! The seeds are planted and now all it takes is time for them to grow into a field of canola, ready to harvest.
The combine grain tank is nearly full. Open the pipe, drive alongside the tipper at the edge of the field, and empty the grain tank into it.
First look around the area and then move up to the tractor.

Cultivating has loosened the soil, making it well prepared for sowing.
You’ll need a tractor and sowing machine for this task. Make sure to fill the sowing machine with seeds.

Since you have two tools attached, you can switch between them to operate them individually. The schematic view of the tractor in the top left displays the tool that’s currently active.
Select the cultivator at the back and lower it into the soil, then drive up and down the field as straight as you can.
Oh, I nearly forgot! You can turn on the radio to listen to some music while you work and enjoy driving from a different perspective...

First, fill the sowing machine with the seed pallet next to it. Then turn it on so it can plant seeds. Finally, lower it into the soil.
Once all this is done, drive up the field again and sow some canola seeds!
First, a combine must be unfolded. Then you can turn it on. This way, the cutter is lowered automatically. You can also raise and lower it manually, but remember to keep it low for harvesting.
Here's the gas pedal, next to it the brake, and you've seen a steering wheel before, haven't ya? It's easy!

In today's lesson, you'll learn about arable farming.
To produce crops, there are three mandatory steps: 1) Cultivating 2) Sowing 3) Harvesting
Let's start with cultivating.

After you have harvested a field, you'll need to prepare it for the next cycle. You can do that quickly by using a cultivator just as before. But from time to time you should plow your field to make sure that it stays as fertile as possible.

After some time has passed, a field is ready to be harvested. In Career, look in the map menu to see if any fields are ready to be harvested.
You need a combine-harvester and header for this task. The combine-harvester harvests the grain and separates it from the stems. Then, the grain can be sold.
Learn how to make and transport bales.
Get in your tractor and attach the weight and bale collector. Then lower the loading platform and drive it into a bale to pick it up.

Hay is obtained by mowing grass and drying it. It's used to feed your cows or sheep.
Straw is obtained after harvesting wheat or barley. It’s used as bedding for your pigs and cows.
Let's make some square straw bales. Get in the tractor and attach the baler.
Great! You know more about bales now and how to make them.
Nice job! If you don't have any animals, you can sell bales to earn cash.
Alright, ready for operation! Now drive over the windrow to collect the straw.
Now, unfold the tool and lower the pick-up. Then, turn it on and drive over the windrow to collect the straw and create your bales.

Good job, that's enough for now. Drive to the place marked on your map to sell the bales.
You’ll have to press the unload button twice: - Once to go into unload mode - Once more to finally unload the bales.

In this lesson, you'll learn all about bales.
Bales are compressed bundles of straw or hay that are easy to transport.
Depending on your baler, you can create round or square bales.

Now you need to pick up your bales and transport them.
Most farmers use a front loader, a bale spike and a bale trailer. But for this lesson, we’ll let you use a bale collector, which simplifies the work tremendously.
Learn how to use a weeder and a sprayer to protect your crops from invasive weeds.
Get in the tractor and attach the weight and the weeder.
Perfect! The weeds have withered and will decompose, allowing your crops to grow faster.
Good! Now, your crops have more room to grow and they won't have to compete with weeds for nutrients.
Sometimes you might be late and the weeds have grown tall, so a weeder can't remove them. In this case, you'll need to use a sprayer and apply plant protection.
For the sprayer, make sure to fill it with herbicide and unfold it. Then, turn it on and drive over the field to spray the weeds.
Unfold the weeder and lower it to the ground, then drive over the field to remove the weeds.
Once the plants have sprouted you can remove weeds by using a weeder in the early growth stages, be careful that they don't grow too tall!

After sowing your crop, you’ll see weeds start growing at the same time as the crop. If you leave it this way, the weeds will reduce the yield of your crop.
You can remove weeds using a: 1) Weeder 2) Sprayer.
Learn how to fertilize your fields to get the maximum yield.

Try it! Go to your tractor.
Attach the weight and cultivator and cultivate the field as you do usually.
Very good! You have improved the yield using fertilizer. Next, we will look at using another way to improve yield: catch crops.
There ya go! Another way to fertilize the soil organically.

Well done! You learned how to increase the yield with organic and manufactured (synthetic) fertilizer, and learned about catch crops.
In the next lesson you’ll learn how to take good care of your fields.
If necessary, make sure to unfold the tool. Then, turn it on and drive across the field to spread the fertilizer.

The most basic way to improve a crop yield is to apply fertilizer.
There are many ways you can fertilize your fields using tools like:
1) Manure spreaders 2) Slurry tanks 3) Fertilizer spreaders
Manure and slurry come from your cows and pigs. Manufactured fertilizer, solid or liquid, can be bought in the shop.
In this lesson, we’ll give you access to every type of tool. Get in a tractor, attach the weight and the tool you want to use.

Oilseed radish is a catch crop, a fast-growing crop you plant BEFORE the crop you intend to harvest.
Once the radishes have started growing, you need to break and burrow them into the ground with a cultivator. This destroys them but adds a lot of organic matter and nutrients to the soil.
Learn how to fell trees, cut them down with a chainsaw and a harvester, and sell the logs.

There are two tools for cutting down trees: - Chainsaw - Tree harvester
Let's start with the chainsaw.

Move close to the tree highlighted with a blue marker.
Position the chainsaw to the side of the tree. When a green circular highlight appears, activate the chainsaw and wait until it has gone through the entire width of the trunk.

Good! Now align your chainsaw vertically and cut a small log (about 1 meter)
You can also remove the branches using the chainsaw.

Great!
Remember, the price of a log of wood depends on its length and straightness. Small, bent pieces sell for less than long straight ones.

Good! It's easy to cut down a tree with a chainsaw but small logs of wood are worth less.
Let's try a tree harvester.
Perfect! Now we will see how to transport and sell the logs.
Well done! We’ll wait a bit until the trees have grown.

You can use the chainsaw to cut down trees, make small logs and move them by hand.
Get your chainsaw out, then rotate it horizontally.

Use the arm to grab a log of wood and put it in the trailer.
Once you’ve finished, drive over to the wood selling point marked on your map.

Tree harvesters are expensive and can only fell trees with a straight trunk. But they can make longer logs that are worth more money.
Get in the harvester.

Drive to a tree and turn on the harvester.
Place the claw in front of the tree and cut it down.

When the tree is in the claw, you can cut it into smaller logs.
Try it.

Light pieces of wood can be moved by hand. Once you have picked it up, you can rotate it.
Grab a log and put it in your truck.

To plant trees, you need a tree planter.
Get in the tractor and attach the tree planter and the weight.
Now unload the log at the marked area to sell it by activating the lever.
Make sure to fill the tool. Then lower it and turn on your tree planter. Drive across the field to plant trees.

Good! Now fasten the tension belts and transport this log to the sawmill.
Tension belts secure your cargo and stop it moving around during transport.

A forwarder is the best tool for transporting logs. It has a crane to grab logs and put them in its trailer.
Let's use it to sell these logs.
First, enter the forwarder.
To stop the forwarder tipping over when we lift a heavy piece of wood, unfold the stabilizers first.

In this last lesson, we’re going to talk about forestry.
You’ll learn to: 1) Plant trees 2) Fell trees 3) Transport and sell wood
Learn how to give your fields the best soil for growing crops.

As usual, go to your tractor, attach the weight and spreader
Then turn it on and drive over your field.
Right then! Tractor. Plow. Weight. Let's go!

Great!
For some crops, you should also plow after the harvest. Let's take a look.
Good! Remember, to avoid a 30% yield malus, you must: - Spread lime after 3 harvests (15% for lime) - Plow after corn, potatoes, sugar beets and sugarcane harvest (15% for plowing)
Welcome to the soil care lesson! In this lesson you’ll learn how to take optimal care of your soil to get the best crop yield.

After three harvest cycles, your field needs lime.
Lime can be bought in pallets from the shop and is spread over the field with a spreader.
After harvesting corn, potatoes, sugar beet and sugarcane, it's a good idea to plow the field.
Now, remove the harvester from the silo and get down and cover the silo.
Learn how to optimize your cows' milk production.

Get in the forage harvester and attach the header and tipper.
The tipper is important because a forage harvester doesn't have internal tanks.

Ok! Your tipper is full.
Go to the bunker silo marked on your map. There’s already some chaff in it.

Well done! For silage, you'll need to change the bale fork for the shovel.
Then, get out of the telehandler and open the silo so you can collect the silage.
Pierce the bales and put them in the mixer.
Alright, get back in the telehandler and fill the mixer wagon with silage until it’s full.

Great!
The chaff will ferment and turn into silage in a few days. Then you can use it to make TMR.

Silage is made by fermenting chaff in a bunker silo.
Chaff is leftover material chopped up by a forage harvester.
You can obtain chaff from corn, wheat, barley or canola.

Great work!
TMR is the best food for your cows.
If you don't have any cows, you can sell silage for a good price at the biogas plant.

Your silage is ready now and we can make TMR.
Remember, you have to mix together hay, straw and silage
Now get in the telehandler and attach the bale fork.

Good job! All the ingredients are now in the mixer wagon.
Enter and activate it to mix the food.
Make sure to unfold the header and open the pipe. Then turn on the forage harvester and drive across the field to make some chaff.

Unload the chaff into the bunker silo.
When the silo is at least 10% full, you can pack it down by driving over it repeatedly.
The TMR is ready now. Put it in the trough.

In this lesson, you’ll learn how to make Total Mixed Ration (TMR).
TMR is the best thing to feed your cows and maximize their milk production.

TMR is produced by mixing together hay, straw and silage in a mixer-wagon.
You should already know how to make hay and straw bales. Let's learn about silage.

You start with substantial funds but no land, no buildings and no equipment. It’s up to you to decide what your farm will look like.

Player '%s' wants to sleep. Accept request?

This is your home. After eight o'clock at night you can come here to sleep until morning.
Note that your helpers also rest and will not work during this time. They’ll resume work in the morning.
It’s better to finish all your work before you sleep because the crops continue to grow and could even spoil if they’re in their final growth stage during this time.
